# Development of new data contract


::: {.callout-tip}
- Read the chapter about [configuration files and data contract](../modules/configuration.qmd) in the architecture documentation before reading this chapter.

- The list of readily available keywords usable for the data contract and configuration files is in the [Standards yaml](../appendix/Standards_yaml.qmd) (but it is not exhaustive, we can add new keywords as needed, but then you will have to ensure the rest work correctly).
:::


## Absolute requirments 

**Configuration must contain a file header**
```text
id: "explecit name"

type: "mytype" # <-- then this file must go in the "mytype" subdirectory

metadata: # configuration file metadata
  display_name: "explicit"
  category: "type of data/analysis"
  description: "Advanced description"
  version: "1.0"
```

**Data contract specification requirements**
- primary key must be the same for metadata and analysis data
- primary key must be unique in metadata and analysis data
- metadata primary key is mandatory by default AND is used for the join with the analysisdata. 


## Data contract specifications

**Only the fields you want to keep for the display must be specified. MUST be in dictionary format.**

- One column must be the primary key (eg. `sample_id`).
- This is specified in the data contract by setting `is_primary_key: true`. Good practice : put this as the first field in the data contract.

- All columns in the data_schema are mandatory by default. #REVIEW - at least for now 

```text
data_schema: # only the columns you want to keep for the display must be specified
    fields1: {type: "categorical", label: "field1" , is_primary_key: true }
    fields2: {type: "numeric", label: "field2"}
    fields3: {type: "date", label: "field3", format: "%Y-%m-%d" }
    fields4: {type: "categorical", label: "field4", categories: ["cat1", "cat2", "cat3"] }
```

It is possible to specify categories to  allow grouping of variable by type so they can be used only for specific types of graphs. eg. categories: "AMR" " or "ST", "phenotype" etc.

You can specify mandatory fields in the data contract by setting `mandatory: true`. 

For metadata : the primary key is mandatory by default AND is used for the join with the analysisdata. 

```text
metadata_schema:
    fields1: {type: "categorical", label: "field1" , is_primary_key: true }
    fields2: {type: "numeric", label: "field2", mandatory: true}
    fields3: {type: "date", label: "field3", format: "%Y-%m-%d" }
    fields4: {type: "categorical", label: "field4", categories: ["cat1", "cat2", "cat3"] }    
```
In metadata you can specify mandatory fields by setting `mandatory: true`. Any additional column might however be ignored. 

example metadata: 

```text
metadata_schema:
    sample_id: {type: "character", label: "id" , is_primary_key: true }
    species_name: {type: "character", label: "species", mandatory: true}
    sampling_date: {type: "date", label: "sampling date", format: "%Y-%m-%d" }
    country_of_origin: {type: "character", label: "country", mandatory: true}    
    host_species: {type: "character", label: "host species", mandatory: true}    
```

-  REVIEW (and test)we need to fix that actually : Use a prefix for all data columns (e.g., `serotype`, `amr_gene`). This prevents the app from crashing if a user uploads a CSV that also has a column named "Species" or "ID."

## Why categories ? 

Categories can be used for Metadata Mapping (see below) and for Plotting Defaults (see below), and can be used to allow creations of specialized plots. 

This creates a **metadata translation layer** eg. instead of "Look for _bla\_CTX-M-15_,", the code says, "Look for everything categorized as _AMR_ for this species."

- This can be leveraged for the visualisation factory to create specialized plots.
- This enables fast prototyping of adding new species while reusing the sames plotting functions
- This allows fast adjustments (eg. new AMR gene set to look at). Basically easily updatable, even by people who do not understand coding. Updating by config.


## How are data and metadata processed 

- data and metadata are verified by the configuration loader. It inspects: if keys are uniques, if keys can be matches, if defines columns are present. If columns are redundant, it will append a suffix to the metadata column name. 
- the configuration loader also inspects if the data contract is valid. It will return errors if the data contract is not respected.
